key_points: Reed Johnson and Churchill are common freshman placements for men
North Weible dorm for women is newly renovated and highly recommended
High-rise dorms house older students; freshman dorms are separate but connected
key_quotes: Our son his freshman year was at Reed Johnson which is all male... Many freshman are put in this along with Churchill and Stockbridge. High rises more older
for women, definitely North Weible! It's newly renovated and I met so many of my closest friends there

key_points: Football culture dominates campus identity overwhelmingly
Campus is described as active with constant student events
Smaller class sizes help students build real relationships with peers and professors
key_quotes: Campus is active and always has students doing things
do not go to NDSU because all you will hear about is their football team, trust I'm from a city near Fargo, and it's annoying as hell
I've been at NDSU since 2019 and I love it

key_points: No specific dining hall complaints surfaced in the data
Bowling, billiards, and esports mentioned as campus activity options
No strong praise or criticism of food quality from any source
key_quotes: Bowling, billiards, esports I think are all 1 credit
Floral design is a really easy 2 credit course

key_points: One student from NJ described moving to NDSU as deeply stressful
Student admitted NDSU was a last-resort choice, causing resentment
Brutal winters flagged repeatedly as a mental health risk
key_quotes: Moving here stressed me out a lot, I didn't want to come to NDSU at all, it was one of my last choices for school
Visit before you make a decision - winter in central IL is a totally different ballgame from winter in ND. It's literally so cold that the snow squeaks when you step on it for most of Dec-March

key_points: Minnesota residents can access in-state tuition via reciprocity agreement
North Star Promise scholarship accepted for MN residents under $80k income
Staff children get 50% tuition discount, a significant perk
key_quotes: North Dakota State University is doing the same thing, with Minnesota and North Dakota residences
originally, it was 27k, but since he is a staff member, it went down to 12k, with the half off tuition thing
Minnesota has reciprocity agreements with North Dakota, South Dakota, Wisconsin, and Manitoba

key_points: Engineering programs widely praised; civil and mechanical both have strong reputations
Calc 1 flagged as the one genuinely brutal course on campus
Faculty described as caring and accessible; small class sizes help
key_quotes: every place I've applied to or worked for wanted NDSU grads digitally as they know the program is much harder and more intense than other tri-state schools
All band teachers are fun only class that's kinda not fun for anyone due to professors is calc and it's one of the hardest class
I've been at NDSU since 2019 and I love it... you can have a solid relationship with professors due to small classes

key_points: 95% acceptance rate means entry is easy; engineering slightly more selective
Honors Program invite comes automatically but has unclear requirements
International students report slow communication and confusion post-application
key_quotes: NDSU doesn't care about test scores unless you are going for scholarships
NDSU is not a particularly difficult school to get into. It had a 95% acceptance rate last year
After the interview professor agreed to supervise me but since then haven't heard from professor through mail it's been 3 weeks

key_points: Fargo winters are brutal: snow squeaks underfoot Dec-March, temps below 10F
Business school is off main campus downtown, requires shuttle
Campus hyperlapse video drew strong alumni nostalgia and positive reactions
key_quotes: winter in ND... It's literally so cold that the snow squeaks when you step on it for most of Dec-March. 10F, -15 wind chill
NDSU has a solid mid-major business school. It's in its own building downtown away from the main campus, but there's a shuttle that runs back and forth constantly
WOW! I just wanna hop on a plane and go and get a Masters degree there. Good times there during my undergrad!

key_points: Civil engineering grads actively sought by employers over regional competitors
Avionics and engineering companies recruit directly on campus
Biology program set one student up well for post-grad opportunities
key_quotes: every place I've applied to or worked for wanted NDSU grads... they know the program is much harder and more intense than other tri-state schools
I recruited for an avionics company for many years at NDSU... Every engineering discipline is top notch
they set me up with a full time job right before I graduated

key_points: In-state and reciprocity tuition makes NDSU one of the cheapest solid engineering options regionally
Staff discount and North Star Promise can cut costs dramatically
Out-of-state students should carefully compare costs before committing
key_quotes: originally, it was 27k, but since he is a staff member, it went down to 12k
I applied to the school my dad attended, NDSU... So huge difference between them
smaller university like NDSU to save money, and possibly head to Twin Cities or Madison's Med School afterward

key_points: Strong alumni loyalty and warmth, especially from engineering and music grads
Football culture is inescapable and polarizing for non-fans
Best fit for Midwest-comfortable students; rough transition for out-of-region students
key_quotes: I love it here with 4 seasons. Yes winter can suck at times but every climate has it's bad moments
do not go to NDSU because all you will hear about is their football team
I've been at NDSU since 2019 and I love it. I love the campus

key_points: **Academic integrity**: Public Reddit post outlines methods to cheat HonorLock exams
**Culture shock**: NJ student described NDSU as last resort, deeply unhappy first semester
**Winter climate**: Multiple warnings that ND winters cause real distress for out-of-state students
key_quotes: Plan B: Figure out how to really use and set up honor lock (pm/chat me if you dont know what im talking about)
Moving here stressed me out a lot, I didn't want to come to NDSU at all
It's literally so cold that the snow squeaks when you step on it for most of Dec-March

key_points: Music department is excellent even for non-majors; marching band highly recommended
Esports lab is a real facility with competitive team opportunities
Easy electives like bowling, billiards, floral design, and popular music exist for credit relief
key_quotes: The music department is great, even for non music majors, and the marching band is a fantastic way to start school
I was on one of the Esports teams at NDSU and a biology major as well
If you need 1 credit, I took bowling my senior year. Joe is a great instructor
